use std::path::Path;

fn write_files(base: &Path, entries: &[(String, Vec<u8>)]) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    std::fs::create_dir_all(base)?;
    for (name, data) in entries {
        let path = base.join(name);
        std::fs::write(path, data)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

fn export_results(output_dir: &Path, results: &[Result]) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    std::fs::create_dir_all(output_dir)?;
    for result in results {
        let filename = format!("{}.txt", result.id);
        std::fs::write(output_dir.join(filename), &result.data)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

struct Result { id: u64, data: Vec<u8> }
